stem: In right triangle XYZ, the right angle is at Z. The length of side YZ is 9, and tanX=940. What is the perimeter of triangle XYZ?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. In right triangle XYZ with the right angle at Z, side YZ is opposite angle X and side XZ is adjacent to angle X. From tan X = YZ/XZ = 9/XZ = 9/40, it follows that XZ = 40. By the Pythagorean theorem, the hypotenuse XY = √(9² + 40²) = √(81 + 1600) = √1681 = 41. The perimeter is YZ + XZ + XY = 9 + 40 + 41 = 90.

Choice A (58) is 9 + 40 + 9, mistakenly reusing YZ as the hypotenuse. Choice B (120) is the perimeter scaled incorrectly. Choice D (98) adds a hypotenuse of 49 instead of 41 (treating 9 + 40 as the hypotenuse).
